phrasal_verbs.meaning: make sb feel sad or lose hope
suggest that sth is the result of
make a place difficult or impossible to enter, leave or communicate with; disconnect
start and be likely to continue for a while (for rain, winter, an economic depression, etc)
become less noisy, powerful or active
represent (for abbreviations and symbols); put up with
destroy or remove (for buildings, statues, etc)
accept sth and try to deal with it

questions.sentence: Local authorities have proposed building a large (1) **puddle / reservoir** to supply freshwater to the growing coastal town...
...the economic and social benefits of (2) **flooding / pouring** the new catchment area will greatly outweigh the costs.
Several small (3) **rural / urban** settlements in the region – two hamlets and four smallholdings – will be relocated, however.
A hiker was struck by (4) **lightning / thunder** on Saturday afternoon...
...while crossing one of the open (5) **plains / fields** near the summit.
It was only (6) **drizzling / showering** when she set out...
...and she hadn't checked the weather (7) **prediction / forecast**...
...the facility has been releasing industrial (8) **waste / litter**, which is increasing pollution...
...in the (9) **suburban / surrounding** farmland.
We're encouraging all visitors to (10) **reuse / repeat** their water bottles when possible...
A researcher from Davington College has sparked debate by arguing that (11) **global / worldwide** warming is not primarily driven by human damage...
...to the (12) **weather / environment**.
Large-scale shifts in the Atlantic (13) **land / climate**, for example, have occurred naturally ever since the planet was formed.
...we certainly weren't responsible for the fact that the mammoths became (14) **extinct / endangered**...
...if industrial chimneys stopped releasing (15) **smoke / fog** into the atmosphere, the (16) **air / wind** we'd all be breathing would be much (17) **cleaner / clearer**, she argued.

B.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
B.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the phrasal verbs in the box.

questions.sentence: The storm should have ...... by tomorrow morning, shouldn't it?
Do you think the waves have ...... enough for us to go surfing safely?
Local activists are ...... a ban on single-use plastic bags in the region.
Why can't they ...... the fact that their policies are harming the local ecosystem?

We'll have to ...... the outdoor film screening if the weather is really terrible, won't we?

It didn't take the team long to ...... the old community hall, did it?

C.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
C.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: Don't throw those old batteries ....... They're not biodegradable!
Cloudy, grey mornings always ...... me down.

D.title: Key word transformation (Phrases and collocations)
D.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

questions.sentence:
Emma isn't feeling very well today.
**weather** Emma is feeling a bit ...... today.

Air pollution has badly damaged people's health in this city.
**effect** Air pollution has ...... people's health in this city.

Would you mind quickly looking at this report?
**look** Would you mind ...... this report?
questions.answer: taking a quick look at/having a quick look at

questions.sentence:
Sorting out the recycling took ages.
**long** It ...... sort out the recycling.

It's Jamie's job to check the water levels every morning.
**responsibility** Jamie ...... the water levels every morning.

There's no point trying to stop him from littering.
**waste** It ...... trying to stop him from littering.

We'll soon be able to see the coastline, won't we?
**sight** We'll soon be ...... the coastline, won't we?

I couldn't steer the kayak because the current was so strong.
**control** I ...... the kayak because the current was so strong.
The days of this river being one of the most polluted in the region have **come / gone** to an end.
Our new apartment is **at / on** the eighth floor.
The council has **made / done** a complete mess of the recycling programme.
It's so humid, I think I'm going to **do / have** a cold shower to cool down.
As usual, so-called environmentalists are **making / taking** a fuss about minor details.
I can't believe there's anyone in the **complete / whole** country who supports this harmful policy.
It looks **like / as** several more species will be at risk of extinction by the end of the decade.
You used to believe there was buried treasure **at / in** the end of every old map trail, didn't you?

F.title: Word patterns (gap fill)
F.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: Visitors to Reykjavik are rarely disappointed (1) ...... the city's unique combination of dramatic landscapes and vibrant culture.
But the city is more famous (2) ...... its proximity to natural wonders such as geysers and the Northern Lights.
It's hard (3) ...... imagine a more spectacular natural setting for a capital city.
Residents are all familiar (4) ...... the volcanic landscape, and are aware (5) ...... the potential risks that come with living in such an active geological zone.
Yet nothing, it seems, will prevent people (6) ...... settling in this remarkable city.
A quick glance (7) ...... a photograph of Reykjavik reveals colourful buildings and modern structures, many of them completely covered (8) ...... snow in winter.
Geologists are constantly warning residents (9) ...... the possibility of a major volcanic eruption.
They expect a significant event (10) ...... occur sooner rather than later.
But Icelanders are not short (11) ...... resilience or resourcefulness.
Except (12) ...... taking sensible precautions and building to the highest safety standards, they carry on with their daily lives as if the natural world around them were perfectly tame.
